untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of: (1) officially announced and legally enforced price ceiling (not available in price/technical indicators), (2) supply/demand fundamental tightness (no shortage indicators in allowed set), (3) price proximity to a dynamic ceiling level (requires external regime parameter not in standard indicators). The core logic depends on exogenous policy regime and fundamental data unavailable in technical indicator set. Close price >= 98 represents proximity to ceiling, but entry conditions 1, 3, and 4 cannot be mechanically expressed without fundamental/policy data feeds.
translation_notes: Attempted partial translation using close price as proxy for ceiling proximity (98 = 98% of ceiling). Exit conditions use +0.5% buffer above assumed ceiling of 100 and 2% profit target. However, the signal's fundamental premise—trading an artificial price control—requires detection of policy regime and supply/demand metrics outside the scope of technical indicators. A complete implementation would require: (a) external flag for 'price_ceiling_active', (b) fundamental shortage/tightness score, (c) official ceiling level as input parameter. Current JSON captures price mechanics only.
